title: eryngium phyteumae
intro: Eryngium phyteumae, also known as the sea daisy or sea holly, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Eryngium phyteumae include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Eryngium phyteumae can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
